MYGA Customer Analysis

Paul Tyler

April 3, 2016

Overview

During both MYGA specials, iPipeline retained all answers to suitability questions answered by clients. This gives us a very interesting look into the demographics of these policyholders. The analysis pulls from file with over 600,000 records of all apps submitted through the Affirm platform. Some of the records include cases that may not have been placed and may possibly include some test cases. We will progressively clean the file as we receive better information from Dell and FGL systems.

Sales Volume

This file includes records of 5,970 applications that represented $532.5 million in premiums. Each sale averaged just under $90,000. The median case size was $50,000. Though we saw a wide range of case sizes, we did see four meaningful clusters at $25,000, $50,000, $100,000 and $150,000.

x
Cases 5970
Total_Premium 532508630
Avg_Premium 89197
Med_Premium 50000

Net Worth of Consumers

A few customers reported very large networths and pushed the average networth to $1.5 million. We think the median networth of slightly less than $1 million - $995,000 - presents a truer picture. Roughly half the assets are liquid and the other half is illiquid.
x
mean_assets 1486247
mean_liq_assets 759464
mean_non_liq_assets 725579
median_assets 995000
median_liq_assets 465000
median_non_liq_assets 431500

Source of Net Worth

The largest source of net worth reported by clients was their home, their qualified retirement plan, and their checking or money market account, in that order. The vast majority of the money used to purchase the annuity came from liquid sources.

Avg_Liq_Src Avg_Non_Liq_Src
1 0.93 0.07

Liquid Assets

Most of the liquid net worth comes first from a qualified plan (i.e. IRA, 401(k)) and then from a checking or money market account. Some large outliers in all categories distort the average amount.

Non-Liquid Assets

The house is typically the major non-liquid asset reported by clients.

Home Values

The average value of the home was close to $300,000. The median was exactly $230,000.

x
minimum 0
mean 296288
median 230000
maximum 6000000

Age

We received the age of owners for the latest MYGA program. The average age of our client is 68 and the oldest was 90.

Avg_age Med_age Max_age
1 68.3 68 90

We saw little difference in average case size in any age band.

Discretionary Income

We ask applicants for their monthly income and monthly expenses. The difference or median discretionary income to spend is $2,500.

x
minimum_discr -55000
mean_discr 5410
median_discr 2500
maximum_discr 983000

Work Status

Close to 70% owners as well as any joint owners reported they were “retired.” Slightly more than 30% indicated they were employed.

Owner

ct status n pct

2 2.00 Employed 1843 0.31
3 3.00 Unemployed 45 0.01

ct status n pct

2 2.00 Employed 178 0.27
3 3.00 Unemployed 11 0.02
4 4.00 Other 16 0.02

Gender

In the last wave of sales, 45% of customers were women, 55% were men. Cases sold to men were slightly higher larger than those sold to women.

Insured.Gender Avg_age Avg_case Total_sales Pct_sales Tot_Cases Pct_Cases Mean_Home Mean_Assets
1 F 68 74551 71345095 0.44 957 0.48 265675 1346541
2 M 68 86697 89297750 0.56 1030 0.52 289100 1588697

Impact of Accepting 1035s

We launched our second online MYGA program begining November 30, 2015 as cash-only offer. On January 20, 2016, we began accepting 1035 exchanges. The change in process did not appear to impact the size of cases. However, the daily sales level picked up substantially after the announcement. The rate hike from 3% to 3.15% on 2/26 created the very large spike in March.

Time Cases Avg_Premium Med_Premium Avg_Assets Med_Assets
1 Post-1035s 1467 81389 50000 1401544 1000000
2 Pre-1035s 520 79318 50000 1671025 991000

That said, accepting 1035 exchanges allowed us to increase the size of cases by approximately 20%.

Is.1035 Cases Total_Premium Avg_Premium Med_Premium Avg_Liq_Src Avg_Non_Liq_Src
1 No 1425 109218651 76645 50000 0.95 0.05
2 Yes 562 51424195 91502 60071 0.88 0.12

Role of the Agent

It appears that agents generally sold the same size of case during the last special. No segment of agents seems to have consistently sold larger cases than the overall population.

## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = agents$St_dev ~ agents$Avg_case_size)
## 
## Coefficients:
##          (Intercept)  agents$Avg_case_size
